leagueBriefing: The World Baseball Classic semifinals are set after a night of high-stakes drama in Miami and Houston. The Dominican Republic continued their path of destruction with a mercy-rule blowout, while Team USA survived a late-inning scare against their northern neighbors to set up a dream matchup between the tournament's two heavyweights.

games.headline: Dominican juggernaut mercy-rules Korea to punch semifinal ticket
games.brief: The Dominican Republic looked every bit the tournament favorite on Friday, dismantling South Korea in a game that was over before the eighth inning could even begin. Behind a dominant start from Cristopher Sánchez and a relentless lineup that has now scored 51 runs in five games, the Dominicans never let Korea breathe. The night ended in spectacular fashion when Austin Wells launched a three-run, walk-off homer in the seventh, triggering the WBC’s mercy rule and sending the Miami crowd into a frenzy.
bullets: Cristopher Sánchez was untouchable, racking up eight strikeouts and allowing only two hits over five scoreless innings.
Austin Wells provided the exclamation point with a pinch-hit, three-run home run to end the game early via the 10-run mercy rule.
The Dominican Republic tied the WBC tournament record with their 14th home run, matching the mark set by Mexico in 2009.
Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and Juan Soto electrified the stadium with aggressive, acrobatic slides into home plate to spark early scoring rallies.
games.context: The undefeated Dominicans advance to face the United States in a highly anticipated semifinal clash in Miami.

games.headline: Team USA survives late Canadian surge to reach WBC semifinals
games.brief: It wasn't the dominant performance many expected from a lineup of MLB superstars, but Team USA did just enough to keep their title defense alive. After jumping out to a comfortable 5-0 lead behind a sharp Logan Webb, the Americans had to sweat out a furious sixth-inning rally from Canada. A two-run shot by Bo Naylor brought the Canadians within striking distance, but the U.S. bullpen—led by a high-leverage escape from David Bednar and a dominant ninth from Mason Miller—bolted the door shut to secure the trip to Miami.
bullets: Logan Webb set the tone early for the U.S., tossing 4.2 scoreless innings with five strikeouts before the bullpen took over.
Canada’s Bo Naylor brought the game to life in the sixth with a towering two-run home run off the second deck in right field.
David Bednar escaped a massive no-out, two-on jam in the seventh inning by striking out the final two batters to preserve the lead.
Mason Miller touched 103 mph while striking out the side in the ninth inning to earn the save and eliminate Canada.
games.context: The U.S. moves on to face the juggernaut Dominican Republic in a semifinal rematch of the 2017 classic.
